vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1033

plan.title: triage:bootstrap / cache:fetch-all / scm:issue:list cannot filter ingest by label

narratives.Description: The triage ingest surface (scm:issue:list, cache:fetch-all, triage:bootstrap) cannot scope ingestion by label or by author, so an operator is forced to pull the entire open backlog. This story adds composable --label and --author filters wired through the scm shim, fetch-all, and bootstrap, and closes #1033 (label) and #1055 (author) together.
narratives.Overview:
## Symptom

`task triage:bootstrap` and the underlying `task cache:fetch-all` / `task scm:issue:list` surface have **no `--label` filter**. Operators who want to ingest only a subset of a backlog (e.g. only `bug`-labelled issues) cannot do it through the canonical surface; they must drop down to ad-hoc `gh api repos/.../issues?labels=...` loops driven by `task cache:put` per-issue.

## Reproduction

On master @ HEAD (deftai/directive), 2026-05-11:

```pwsh
task triage:bootstrap -- --repo deftai/directive --help
# argparse surface: --project-root / --repo / --limit / --state / --batch-size /
#                   --delay-ms / --fetch-timeout-s / --quiet / --json
# No --label / --labels / --include-label / --exclude-label.

task cache:fetch-all -- --source github-issue --repo deftai/directive --help
# Same: no label filter; only --batch-size / --delay-ms / --ttl-seconds / --state / --limit.
```

Confirm by reading the source:

- `scripts/triage_bootstrap.py::_build_parser` — args end at `--json`; no label surface.
- `scripts/cache.py::_cmd_fetch_all` → `cache_fetch_all` — forwards only `state` / `limit` to `_cache_fetch.run_fetch_all`.
- `scripts/_cache_fetch.py::scm_list_issues` — invokes `task scm:issue:list -- --repo OWNER/REPO --state STATE --limit N --json number,title,state,updatedAt`. The `gh issue list` / REST `/repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues` wrapper underneath natively accepts `--label <name>` (gh) / `?labels=<csv>` (REST), but the wrapper does not expose it.

Ad-hoc workaround (used in the 2026-05-11 session that surfaced this gap):

```pwsh
$repo = "deftai/directive"
$nums = gh api "repos/$repo/issues?labels=bug&state=open&per_page=100" `
        --jq '[.[] | select(.pull_request == null) | .number] | sort' | ConvertFrom-Json
foreach ($n in $nums) {
    $tmp = [System.IO.Path]::GetTempFileName()
    gh api "repos/$repo/issues/$n" > $tmp
    task cache:put -- github-issue "$repo/$n" --raw-file $tmp
    Remove-Item $tmp
}
```

This works but bypasses the orchestrator entirely — no rate-limit handling, no 429 retry, no TTL skip-fresh idempotency, no batch / delay knobs, no recap.

## Why this matters

- The framework already does targeted ingest for actions / actors (vBRIEF, triage actions), but the *intake* layer is unfiltered. Large backlogs (1000+ issues) force operators into a single all-or-nothing run.
- Cohort triage workflows (e.g. "rebootstrap the bug subset after a labeller pass") have no surface; the alternative is `cache:fetch-all` over the entire backlog and rely on TTL skip-fresh, which still pays one `scm:issue:view` per fresh issue.
- The `deft-directive-gh-triage` skill description targets bugs specifically, but there's no upstream filter to feed it a bug-only candidate set.

## Recommended remediation paths

1. **Plumb `--label` through the four layers:**
   - `scripts/scm.py issue list` accepts `--label NAME` (repeatable) → forwards to `gh issue list --label NAME` (and `?labels=...` for `--rest`).
   - `_cache_fetch.scm_list_issues(repo, state, limit, labels=None)` forwards the flag.
   - `cache_fetch_all(..., labels=None)` accepts and passes through.
   - `scripts/triage_bootstrap.py::_build_parser` exposes `--label` (repeatable) forwarded via the same plumbing path the existing `--limit` / `--state` use.

2. **Alternative: `--candidate-set` file input.** Accept a newline-separated list of issue numbers; orchestrator skips `scm:issue:list` entirely and walks the supplied set. More general than `--label` (covers "bug + perf" intersections, milestone subsets, etc.).

3. **Cheapest interim:** document the ad-hoc workaround above in `docs/` so operators stop re-deriving it. Doesn't close the gap but lowers the surprise cost until #1.

## Suggested scope split

- Story A — `--label` plumbing (option 1 above). Single PR.
- Story B — `--candidate-set` plumbing (option 2). Separate PR; depends on Story A's argparse / orchestrator refactor.
- Story C — docs surface (option 3). Lands alongside Story A.

## Refs

- Session 2026-05-11 surfaced this when a user asked "do a bootstrap of all issues with bug labels"; the agent fell back to the ad-hoc loop above.
- `scripts/triage_bootstrap.py`
- `scripts/cache.py::cache_fetch_all`
- `scripts/_cache_fetch.py::scm_list_issues`
- `tasks/scm.yml::issue:list`
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
- Add --label and --author filter options across the triage ingest surface so cache:fetch-all and triage:bootstrap can scope ingestion the way scm:issue:list already partially supports: wire the filters through scripts/scm.py (issue list query), scripts/cache.py + scripts/_cache_fetch.py (fetch-all), and scripts/triage_bootstrap.py (bootstrap).
- --label accepts comma-separated and repeated flags (merge occurrences); --author accepts a single GitHub login; both pass through to the underlying gh issue list query via the scm shim (no raw gh).
- Add tests covering label-only, author-only, and combined filtering for the scm issue-list helper and the fetch-all / bootstrap pass-through (extend tests/cli/test_scm_smoke.py and the triage bootstrap tests).
- Add a CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] entry in #1242 style. Closes #1033 (label) and #1055 (author).
narratives.UserStory: As an operator bootstrapping a triage cache, I want to filter ingestion by label and by author, so that I can scope cache:fetch-all and triage:bootstrap to the slice of issues I intend to triage instead of pulling the entire open backlog.
narratives.Approach: 2026-06-17 file-overlap audit: #1033 (label) and #1055 (author) hit the IDENTICAL ingest surface (scm:issue:list / cache:fetch-all / triage:bootstrap), so they are combined into one carrier and one PR to avoid a self-collision. scm.py already has partial --label handling ('additional filters belong on #881'); this story wires label through fetch-all/bootstrap and adds --author end-to-end. Disjoint from the other cohort carriers. All gh access stays behind scripts/scm.py (verify:scm-boundary). Parallel-safe; no depends_on.

items.title: --label filter wired through cache:fetch-all and triage:bootstrap.

narrative.Acceptance: Given --label NAME (comma-separated or repeated), when cache:fetch-all / triage:bootstrap run, then only issues carrying the label(s) are ingested (asserted in the triage bootstrap / scm tests).

items.title: --author filter added across scm:issue:list, cache:fetch-all, and triage:bootstrap.

narrative.Acceptance: Given --author LOGIN, when the ingest surface runs, then only issues authored by LOGIN are ingested (asserted in tests).
Given both --label and --author, when ingest runs, then only issues matching both are ingested (asserted in tests).

items.title: gh access stays behind the scm shim.

narrative.Acceptance: Given the diff, when task verify:scm-boundary runs in task check, then no raw gh call is introduced outside scripts/scm.py.
Given the test suite and diff, when reviewed, then label/author/combined filtering is covered by tests and CHANGELOG.md has an [Unreleased] entry referencing #1033 and #1055.

references.title: Issue #1033: triage:bootstrap / cache:fetch-all / scm:issue:list cannot filter ingest by label
Issue #1055: triage:bootstrap / cache:fetch-all / scm:issue:list cannot filter ingest by author

expected_outputs: combined label+author filtering composes; tests cover all three
swarm.missing_traces_justification: Two sibling bugs (#1033 label, #1055 author) combined into one carrier; identical surface. No spec-section trace applies.
